title: Visual Web Tool - Graph Schema (Multi-Model)
description: Schema describing multiple graphs (models) of nodes and paths with optional basic visual layout hints and optimization parameters. Models are organized as a named dictionary indexed by model ID.

models.description: Dictionary of statistical models indexed by model ID; each model contains its own nodes, paths, and optimization configuration

optimization.description: Optional model-specific optimization configuration including parameter type definitions, fit function, and missing data handling

fitFunction.description: Fit function for parameter estimation. Defaults to ML if not specified.

missingness.description: Strategy for handling missing data. This is a hint; the backend may use different strategies for different data types (e.g., covariance matrices have no missing data). Defaults to FIML for raw data if not specified.

parameterTypes.description: Semantic categories for path parameters with default priors, bounds, and starting values

bounds.description: Lower and upper bounds [min, max]; null means unbounded

start.description: Starting value for optimization; 'auto' for automatic initialization

variableCharacteristics.description: For variable nodes: semantic characteristics defining how the variable behaves structurally

manifestLatent.description: Whether the variable is observed (manifest) or unobserved (latent). When specified, locks the display type, overriding auto-inference from database paths. Omit to auto-infer: manifest if dataset path exists, latent otherwise.

exogeneity.description: Whether the variable is externally determined (exogenous) or internally determined by the model (endogenous)

customTags.description: User-defined tags for additional classification or metadata

levelOfMeasurement.description: Level of measurement for multilevel models (e.g., 'within', 'between')

tags.description: Optional list of tags for classification/metadata. Backwards-compatible with single `tag` string.

visual.description: Optional visual layout hints; coordinates are relative to model origin (user units).

mappings.description: For dataset nodes: mapping of CSV column names to target node IDs

datasetSource.description: For dataset nodes: metadata and source of the associated data (file-based or embedded)

type.description: Whether data is stored in an external file or embedded in the schema

location.description: For type='file': path to the data file (relative to schema directory unless absolute). For type='embedded': unused.

format.description: Format of the external data file. For embedded data, always 'json'.

encoding.description: Character encoding of the file (e.g., 'UTF-8'). Defaults to 'UTF-8' if not specified.

columnTypes.description: Mapping of column names to JSON-appropriate data types for type coercion on load

object.description: For type='embedded': array of row objects with column names as keys. For type='file': unused.

md5.description: MD5 hash of the file (for file type) for integrity verification

provenance.description: Model history and fit results tracking

lastModified.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last schema modification

structureHash.description: Hash of fitting-relevant structure (nodes, paths, optimization params). Used to detect staleness of fit results.

fitResults.description: Array of fit results from running this model with different backends or configurations. Most recent fit is last.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this fit was run

backend.description: Which backend was used for fitting

isDirty.description: Whether this fit is stale (model structure has changed since fit was run)

structureHash.description: Hash of model structure at time of fitting. Compare to provenance.structureHash to detect staleness.

statusRemarks.description: Human-readable convergence status message (e.g., 'Convergence detected. Code 0' for OpenMx)

fitValue.description: Fit value (negative log-likelihood, chi-square, or equivalent depending on fitFunction)

degreesOfFreedom.description: Degrees of freedom for the model

sampleSize.description: Number of observations used in fitting

parameters.description: Fitted parameter values, indexed by path ID

standardErrors.description: Standard errors for fitted parameters, indexed by path ID

visualization.description: Model-level visualization configuration: rendering hints for interactive and static layouts. Properties are optional; defaults apply when omitted. Different rendering contexts (RStudio, web, PDF) may interpret these hints differently.

anchor.description: Display offset to apply to all node positions before rendering. When null, automatically computed from node bounds and margins. When specified as {x, y}, adds this offset to all canonical node coordinates for display only (canonical coordinates in nodes[].visual remain unchanged). Used to center or position the model in different contexts (interactive canvas, PDF page, etc.)

displayContext.description: Rendering hints for different display contexts (interactive, print, web). Defaults: marginLeft=50, marginTop=50, modelWidthFactor=0.5. TODO: Move to configuration for cascade/submodel positioning.

marginLeft.description: Left margin in display units; used when computing default anchor

marginTop.description: Top margin in display units; used when computing default anchor

modelWidthFactor.description: Scaling factor relative to available width (0-1); used when computing default anchor for horizontal centering

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the path; if null, label will not be displayed. For dataset-to-node paths, this should be the CSV column name.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the path

value.description: Numeric value for the path parameter; defaults to 1.0. For dataset-to-node paths, must be null.

free.description: Whether the path parameter is free to vary or fixed to its value; defaults to 'free'. Note: Should not be specified for paths originating from dataset nodes.

parameterType.description: Optional semantic category from optimization.parameterTypes; used to apply default priors, bounds, and starting values

optimization.description: Optional path-specific optimization overrides; merged with defaults from parameterType

bounds.description: Lower and upper bounds [min, max]; null means unbounded (overrides parameterType default)

start.description: Starting value for optimization; 'auto' for automatic initialization (overrides parameterType default)

tags.description: Optional list of tags for classification/metadata. Backwards-compatible with single `tag` string.

midpointOffset.description: Optional offset for two-headed covariance arrows; vector (x,y) in user units applied to the straight-line midpoint
